Operation 'authorize' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks clarity on inputs (authorization-id parameter), outputs (authorization code, state parameter), and side effects (session establishment). Enhanced version specifies the path parameter, expected response structure, and the prerequisite nature of this step in the OAuth2 flow for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Initiates the OAuth2 authorization process for PSD2 consent or payment.
data.summary_suggestion: Initiate OAuth2 authorization for PSD2 consent
data.description_suggestion: Initiates the OAuth2 authorization process for PSD2 consent or payment authorization. Accepts an authorization-id path parameter identifying the authorization session. Returns an authorization response containing the authorization code and state parameter for token exchange. Establishes an authorization session that must be completed before access tokens can be obtained.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity on inputs (authorization-id parameter), outputs (authorization code, state parameter), and side effects (session establishment). Enhanced version specifies the path parameter, expected response structure, and the prerequisite nature of this step in the OAuth2 flow for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'cancelAuthorize' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, unclear_purpose. Original summary and description are vague and lack actionable details. Enhanced version clarifies the cancellation action, specifies the authorization-id parameter, explains the side effects (session termination, code invalidation), and indicates the need for a new authorization request. This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: Initiates the OAuth2 cancellation authorization process.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels an active OAuth2 authorization session identified by the authorization-id path parameter. Terminates the authorization process and invalidates any pending authorization codes. Returns a cancellation confirmation response. After cancellation, the authorization session cannot be resumed and a new authorization request must be initiated to obtain access tokens.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are vague and lack actionable details. Enhanced version clarifies the cancellation action, specifies the authorization-id parameter, explains the side effects (session termination, code invalidation), and indicates the need for a new authorization request. This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'createToken' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement to specify required request body structure (TokenRequest schema details), response structure (TokenResponse contents), and the completion of the OAuth2 flow. This provides AI agents with clear understanding of required inputs and expected outputs.

data.current_summary: Exchange authorization code for access token
data.current_description: Exchange the authorization code provided in the authorization response for an access token.
data.description_suggestion: Exchanges an authorization code obtained from the authorization endpoint for an OAuth2 access token. Requires a TokenRequest body containing the authorization code, client credentials, and grant type. Returns a TokenResponse containing the access token, token type, expiration time, and refresh token if applicable. This completes the OAuth2 authorization code flow and enables API access.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs enhancement to specify required request body structure (TokenRequest schema details), response structure (TokenResponse contents), and the completion of the OAuth2 flow. This provides AI agents with clear understanding of required inputs and expected outputs.
